gloss.it: He is out of His mind

G1525.brief: I go in, come in, enter
G1525.full: I go in, come in, enter.

G3825.brief: again, further, on the other hand
G3825.full: again, back, once more, further, on the other hand.

G1519.full: into, in, unto, to, upon, towards, for, among.

G4864.full: an assembly, congregation, synagogue, either the place or the people gathered together in the place.

G1563.full: (a) there, yonder, in that place, (b) thither, there.

G444.brief: a man, one of the human race
G444.full: a man, one of the human race.

G3583.full: I dry up, parch, am ripened, wither, waste away.

G846.brief: he, she, it, they, them, same
G846.full: he, she, it, they, them, same.

G2323.brief: I care for, attend, serve, heal
G2323.full: I care for, attend, serve, treat, especially of a physician; hence: I heal.

G3004.full: (denoting speech in progress), (a) I say, speak; I mean, mention, tell, (b) I call, name, especially in the pass., (c) I tell, command.

G1453.full: (a) I wake, arouse, (b) I raise up.

G3319.full: middle, in the middle, between, in the midst of.

G18.full: intrinsically good, good in nature, good whether it be seen to be so or not, the widest and most colorless of all words with this meaning.

G4160.full: (a) I make, manufacture, construct, (b) I do, act, cause.

G2554.full: I do harm, do wrong, do evil, commit sin.

G5590.full: (a) the vital breath, breath of life, (b) the human soul, (c) the soul as the seat of affections and will, (d) the self, (e) a human person, an individual.

G615.full: I put to death, kill; fig: I abolish.

G1161.brief: but, on the other hand, and
G1161.full: a weak adversative particle, generally placed second in its clause; but, on the other hand, and.

G4623.full: I keep silence, am silent, either voluntarily or involuntarily.

G3326.full: (a) gen: with, in company with, (b) acc: (1) behind, beyond, after, of place, (2) after, of time, with nouns, neut. of adjectives.

G4818.full: I am moved to grief by sympathy.

G1909.brief: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
G1909.full: on, to, against, on the basis of, at.

G2588.full: lit: the heart; mind, character, inner self, will, intention, center.

G1614.full: I stretch out (forth), cast forth (as of an anchor), lay hands on.

G600.full: I set up again, restore to its original position or condition; hence: I restore, give back.

G5330.full: a Pharisee, one of the Jewish sect so called.

G2265.brief: the Herodians, the partisans of Herod Antipas
G2265.full: the Herodians, the partisans of Herod (Antipas).

G4824.full: (a) a body of advisers (assessors) in a court, a council, (b) abstr: consultation, counsel, advice; resolution, decree.

G1325.full: I offer, give; I put, place.

G2596.brief: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
G2596.full: gen: against, down from, throughout, by; acc: over against, among, daily, day-by-day, each day, according to, by way of.

G3704.brief: how, in order that, so that
G3704.full: how, in order that, so that, that.

G622.full: (a) I kill, destroy, (b) I lose, mid: I am perishing (the resultant death being viewed as certain).

G2424.full: Jesus; the Greek form of Joshua; Jesus, son of Eliezer; Jesus, surnamed Justus.

G402.full: I return, retire, withdraw, depart (underlying idea perhaps of taking refuge from danger or of going into retirement).

G2281.full: (a) the sea, in contrast to the land, (b) a particular sea or lake, e.g. the sea of Galilee (Tiberias), the Red Sea.

G4128.full: a multitude, crowd, great number, assemblage.

G1056.full: Galilee, a district towards the southern end of the Roman province Syria; the northern division of Palestine.

G2449.full: Judea, a Roman province, capital Jerusalem.

G2414.full: the Greek form of the Hebrew name: Jerusalem.

G2401.full: Idumea, Edom, a district of Arabia, immediately south of Judea.

G4008.full: over, on the other side, beyond.

G2446.full: the Jordan, a great river flowing due south and bounding Galilee, Samaria, and Judea on the east.

G5184.full: Tyre, an ancient city, the capital of Phoenicia.

G4605.full: Sidon, a great coast city of Phoenicia.

G191.full: I hear, listen, comprehend by hearing; pass: is heard, reported.

G3745.brief: how much, how great, how many
G3745.full: how much, how great, how many, as great as, as much.

G4342.full: I persist, persevere in, continue steadfast in; I wait upon.

G1223.full: (a) gen: through, throughout, by the instrumentality of, (b) acc: through, on account of, by reason of, for the sake of, because of.

G3793.full: a crowd, mob, the common people.

G2346.brief: I make narrow, persecute, press hard
G2346.full: (a) I make narrow (strictly: by pressure); I press upon, (b) I persecute, press hard.

G5620.full: so that, therefore, so then, so as to.

G1968.full: I fall upon, press upon, light upon, come over.

G3148.full: (a) a scourge, lash, of leathern thongs with pieces of metal sewn up in them, (b) met: severe pains (sufferings), disease.

G2334.full: I look at, gaze, behold; I see, experience, discern; I partake of.

G4363.brief: I fall down before, beat against
G4363.full: (a) I fall down before, (b) I beat against, rush violently upon.

G3754.full: that, since, because; may introduce direct discourse.

G2008.full: (a) I rebuke, chide, admonish, (b) I warn.

G5318.full: apparent, clear, visible, manifest; adv: clearly.

G305.full: I go up, mount, ascend; of things: I rise, spring up, come up.

G2309.full: I will, wish, desire, am willing, intend, design.

G565.brief: I come or go away from, depart, return
G565.full: I come or go away from, depart, return, arrive, go after, follow.

G1427.full: twelve; the usual way in which the Twelve apostles of Jesus are referred to.

G652.brief: an apostle, a messenger, an envoy, a delegate
G652.full: a messenger, envoy, delegate, one commissioned by another to represent him in some way, especially a man sent out by Jesus Christ Himself to preach the Gospel; an apostle.

G3687.brief: I give a name to, mention
G3687.full: I give a name to, mention, call upon the name of.

G649.brief: I send forth, send, send away, dismiss
G649.full: I send forth, send (as a messenger, commission, etc.), send away, dismiss.

G1849.full: (a) power, authority, weight, especially: moral authority, influence, (b) in a quasi-personal sense, derived from later Judaism, of a spiritual power, and hence of an earthly power.

G1544.brief: I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce
G1544.full: I throw (cast, put) out; I banish; I bring forth, produce.

G1140.full: an evil-spirit, demon; a heathen deity.

G2007.brief: I put, place upon, lay on
G2007.full: I put, place upon, lay on; I add, give in addition.

G2385.full: James, (a) the Small, son of Alphaeus, and one of the Twelve, (b) half-brother of Jesus, (c) father (?) of Jude, (d) son of Zebedee, and brother of John, one of the Twelve, killed A.D. 44.

G2199.full: Zebedee, father of the apostles James and John.

G2491.full: John: the Baptist, the apostle, a member of the Sanhedrin, or John Mark.

G80.full: a brother, member of the same religious community, especially a fellow-Christian.

G406.full: Andrew, brother of Simon Peter, and one of the apostles of Jesus, belonging to Bethsaida.

G5376.full: Philip, (a) one of the twelve apostles of Jesus, (b) tetrarch of the Ituraean and Trachonitic region, half-brother of Herod Antipas, tetrarch of Galilee, (c) one of the seven original deacons at Jerusalem and a missionary.

G918.full: Bartholomew, surname of Nathanael, one of the twelve apostles of Jesus.

G2381.full: Thomas, also called Didymus, one of the Twelve.

G256.full: Alphaeus; apparently two persons, (a) father of Levi, and (b) father of James.

G2280.full: Thaddaeus, one of the twelve apostles.

G3860.full: I hand over, pledge, hand down, deliver, commit, commend, betray, abandon.

G3624.full: (a) a house, the material building, (b) a household, family, lineage, nation.

G4905.brief: I come or go with, accompany, assemble
G4905.full: I come or go with, accompany; I come together, assemble.

G1410.full: (a) I am powerful, have (the) power, (b) I am able, I can.

G5315.full: I eat, partake of food; met: I devour, consume (e.g. as rust does); used only in fut. and 2nd aor. tenses.

G3844.full: gen: from; dat: beside, in the presence of; acc: alongside of.

G2902.full: I am strong, mighty, hence: I rule, am master, prevail; I obtain, take hold of; I hold, hold fast.

G1839.full: (lit: I remove from a standing position), (a) in trans. tenses: I astonish, amaze, (b) in intrans. tenses: I am astonished, amazed; I am out of my mind, am mad.

G1122.brief: a scribe, town-clerk, man of learning
G1122.full: (a) in Jerusalem, a scribe, one learned in the Jewish Law, a religious teacher, (b) at Ephesus, the town-clerk, the secretary of the city, (c) a man of learning generally.

G2597.full: I go down, come down, either from the sky or from higher land, descend.

G954.full: Beelzebul, a name of Satan, the chief of evil spirits.

G758.full: a ruler, governor, leader, leading man; with the Jews, an official member (a member of the executive) of the assembly of elders.

G3850.full: (a) a comparison, (b) a parable, often of those uttered by our Lord, (c) a proverb, an adage.

G4459.full: how, in what manner, by what means.

G932.full: kingship, sovereignty, authority, rule, especially of God, both in the world, and in the hearts of men; hence: kingdom, in the concrete sense.

G3307.full: I divide into parts, divide, part, share, distribute; mid: I share, take part in a partitioning; I distract.

G2476.full: trans: (a) I make to stand, place, set up, establish, appoint; mid: I place myself, stand, (b) I set in balance, weigh; intrans: (c) I stand, stand by, stand still; met: I stand ready, stand firm, am steadfast.

G3614.full: a house, household, dwelling; meton: goods, property, means.

G450.brief: I raise up, set up, rise from
G450.full: I raise up, set up; I rise from among (the) dead; I arise, appear.

G5056.full: (a) an end, (b) event or issue, (c) the principal end, aim, purpose, (d) a tax.

G2478.full: strong (originally and generally of physical strength); mighty, powerful, vehement, sure.

G4632.brief: a vessel to contain liquid, utensil, tackle
G4632.full: a vessel to contain liquid; a vessel of mercy or wrath; any instrument by which anything is done; a household utensil; of ships: tackle.

G1210.full: I bind, tie, fasten; I impel, compel; I declare to be prohibited and unlawful.

G281.brief: verily, truly, amen, so let it be
G281.full: verily, truly, amen; at the end of sentences may be paraphrased by: So let it be.

G3956.brief: all, the whole, every kind of
G3956.full: all, the whole, every kind of.

G863.brief: I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit
G863.full: (a) I send away, (b) I let go, release, permit to depart, (c) I remit, forgive, (d) I permit, suffer.

G987.full: I speak evil against, blaspheme, use abusive or scurrilous language about (God or men).

G302.brief: an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent
G302.full: an untranslatable word (under the circumstances, in that case, anyhow), the general effect of which is to make a statement contingent, which would otherwise be definite: it is thus regularly used with the subjunctive mood.

G40.full: set apart by (or for) God, holy, sacred.

G859.full: a sending away, a letting go, a release, pardon, complete forgiveness.

G165.brief: an age, a cycle of time
G165.full: an age, a cycle (of time), especially of the present age as contrasted with the future age, and of one of a series of ages stretching to infinity.

G1777.full: involved in, held in, hence: liable, generally with dat. (or gen.) of the punishment.

G166.full: age-long, and therefore: practically eternal, unending; partaking of the character of that which lasts for an age, as contrasted with that which is brief and fleeting.

G4739.full: I stand fast, stand firm, persevere.

G2564.full: (a) I call, summon, invite, (b) I call, name.

G2521.full: I sit, am seated, enthroned; I dwell, reside.

G79.full: a sister, a woman (fellow-)member of a church, a Christian woman.

G2212.full: I seek, search for, desire, require, demand.

G611.full: I answer, reply, take up the conversation.

G2307.full: an act of will, will; plur: wishes, desires.

bsb.1: Once again Jesus entered the synagogue, and a man with a withered hand was there.
bsb.2: In order to accuse Jesus, they were watching to see if He would heal on the Sabbath.
bsb.3: Then Jesus said to the man with the withered hand, “Stand up among us.”
bsb.4: And He asked them, “Which is lawful on the Sabbath: to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it?” But they were silent.
bsb.5: Jesus looked around at them with anger and sorrow at their hardness of heart. Then He said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” So he stretched it out, and it was restored.
bsb.6: At this, the Pharisees went out and began plotting with the Herodians how they might kill Jesus.
bsb.7: So Jesus withdrew with His disciples to the sea, accompanied by a large crowd from Galilee, Judea,
bsb.8: Jerusalem, Idumea, the region beyond the Jordan, and the vicinity of Tyre and Sidon. The large crowd came to Him when they heard what great things He was doing.
bsb.9: Jesus asked His disciples to have a boat ready for Him so that the crowd would not crush Him.
bsb.10: For He had healed so many that all who had diseases were pressing forward to touch Him.
bsb.11: And when the unclean spirits saw Him, they fell down before Him and cried out, “You are the Son of God!”
bsb.12: But He warned them sternly not to make Him known.
bsb.13: Then Jesus went up on the mountain and called for those He wanted, and they came to Him.
bsb.14: He appointed twelve of them, whom He designated as apostles, to accompany Him, to be sent out to preach,
bsb.15: and to have authority to drive out demons.
bsb.16: These are the twelve He appointed: Simon (whom He named Peter),
bsb.17: James son of Zebedee and his brother John (whom He named Boanerges, meaning “Sons of Thunder”),
bsb.18: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot,
bsb.19: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
bsb.20: Then Jesus went home, and once again a crowd gathered, so that He and His disciples could not even eat.
bsb.21: When His family heard about this, they went out to take custody of Him, saying, “He is out of His mind.”
bsb.22: And the scribes who had come down from Jerusalem were saying, “He is possessed by Beelzebul, ” and, “By the prince of the demons He drives out demons.”
bsb.23: So Jesus called them together and began to speak to them in parables: “How can Satan drive out Satan?
bsb.24: If a kingdom is divided against itself, it cannot stand.
bsb.25: If a house is divided against itself, it cannot stand.
bsb.26: And if Satan is divided and rises against himself, he cannot stand; his end has come.
bsb.27: Indeed, no one can enter a strong man’s house to steal his possessions unless he first ties up the strong man. Then he can plunder his house.
bsb.28: Truly I tell you, the sons of men will be forgiven all sins and blasphemies, as many as they utter.
bsb.29: But whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven; he is guilty of eternal sin.”
bsb.30: Jesus made this statement because they were saying, “He has an unclean spirit.”
bsb.31: Then Jesus’ mother and brothers came and stood outside. They sent someone in to summon Him,
bsb.32: and a crowd was sitting around Him. “Look,” He was told, “Your mother and brothers are outside, asking for You.”
bsb.33: But Jesus replied, “Who are My mother and My brothers?”
bsb.34: Looking at those seated in a circle around Him, He said, “Here are My mother and My brothers!
bsb.35: For whoever does the will of God is My brother and sister and mother.”

web.1: He entered again into the synagogue, and there was a man there who had his hand withered.
web.2: They watched him, whether he would heal him on the Sabbath day, that they might accuse him.
web.3: He said to the man who had his hand withered, “Stand up.”
web.4: He said to them, “Is it lawful on the Sabbath day to do good, or to do harm? To save a life, or to kill?” But they were silent.
web.5: When he had looked around at them with anger, being grieved at the hardening of their hearts, he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was restored as healthy as the other.
web.6: The Pharisees went out, and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
web.7: Jesus withdrew to the sea with his disciples, and a great multitude followed him from Galilee, from Judea,
web.8: from Jerusalem, from Idumaea, beyond the Jordan, and those from around Tyre and Sidon. A great multitude, hearing what great things he did, came to him.
web.9: He spoke to his disciples that a little boat should stay near him because of the crowd, so that they wouldn’t press on him.
web.10: For he had healed many, so that as many as had diseases pressed on him that they might touch him.
web.11: The unclean spirits, whenever they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, “You are the Son of God!”
web.12: He sternly warned them that they should not make him known.
web.13: He went up into the mountain, and called to himself those whom he wanted, and they went to him.
web.14: He appointed twelve, that they might be with him, and that he might send them out to preach,
web.15: and to have authority to heal sicknesses and to cast out demons:
web.16: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
web.17: James the son of Zebedee; and John, the brother of James, (whom he called Boanerges, which means, Sons of Thunder);
web.18: Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Thaddaeus; Simon the Zealot;
web.19: and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him. Then he came into a house.
web.20: The multitude came together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
web.21: When his friends heard it, they went out to seize him: for they said, “He is insane.”
web.22: The scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebul,” and, “By the prince of the demons he casts out the demons.”
web.23: He summoned them, and said to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?
web.24: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
web.25: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
web.26: If Satan has risen up against himself, and is divided, he can’t stand, but has an end.
web.27: But no one can enter into the house of the strong man to plunder, unless he first binds the strong man; and then he will plunder his house.
web.28: Most certainly I tell you, all sins of the descendants of man will be forgiven, including their blasphemies with which they may blaspheme;
web.29: but whoever may blaspheme against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is subject to eternal condemnation.”
web.30: —because they said, “He has an unclean spirit.”
web.31: His mother and his brothers came, and standing outside, they sent to him, calling him.
web.32: A multitude was sitting around him, and they told him, “Behold, your mother, your brothers, and your sisters are outside looking for you.”
web.33: He answered them, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
web.34: Looking around at those who sat around him, he said, “Behold, my mother and my brothers!
web.35: For whoever does the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.”

asv.1: And he entered again into the synagogue; and there was a man there who had his hand withered.
asv.2: And they watched him, whether he would heal him on the sabbath day; that they might accuse him.
asv.3: And he saith unto the man that had his hand withered, Stand forth.
asv.4: And he saith unto them, Is it lawful on the sabbath day to do good, or to do harm? to save a life, or to kill? But they held their peace.
asv.5: And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved at the hardening of their heart, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thy hand. And he stretched it forth; and his hand was restored.
asv.6: And the Pharisees went out, and straightway with the Herodians took counsel against him, how they might destroy him.
asv.7: And Jesus with his disciples withdrew to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed; and from Judaea,
asv.8: and from Jerusalem, and from Idumaea, and beyond the Jordan, and about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, hearing what great things he did, came unto him.
asv.9: And he spake to his disciples, that a little boat should wait on him because of the crowd, lest they should throng him:
asv.10: for he had healed many; insomuch that as many as had plagues pressed upon him that they might touch him.
asv.11: And the unclean spirits, whensoever they beheld him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.
asv.12: And he charged them much that they should not make him known.
asv.13: And he goeth up into the mountain, and calleth unto him whom he himself would; and they went unto him.
asv.14: And he appointed twelve, that they might be with him, and that he might send them forth to preach,
asv.15: and to have authority to cast out demons:
asv.17: and James the [son] of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and them he surnamed Boanerges, which is, Sons of thunder:
asv.18: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the [son] of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Cananaean,
asv.19: and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him. And he cometh into a house.
asv.20: And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
asv.21: And when his friends heard it, they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is beside himself.
asv.22: And the scribes that came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub, and, By the prince of the demons casteth he out the demons.
asv.23: And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?
asv.24: And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
asv.25: And if a house be divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
asv.26: And if Satan hath rise up against himself, and is divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.
asv.27: But no one can enter into the house of the strong [man], and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong [man]; and then he will spoil his house.
asv.28: Verily I say unto you, All their sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and their blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:
asv.29: but whosoever shall blaspheme against the Holy Spirit hath never forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin:
asv.30: because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.
asv.31: And there come his mother and his brethren; and, standing without, they sent unto him, calling him.
asv.32: And a multitude was sitting about him; and they say unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee.
asv.33: And he answereth them, and saith, Who is my mother and my brethren?
asv.34: And looking round on them that sat round about him, he saith, Behold, my mother and my brethren!
asv.35: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and sister, and mother.

kjv.1: And he entered again into the synagogue; and there was a man there which had a withered hand.
kjv.2: And they watched him, whether he would heal him on the sabbath day; that they might accuse him.
kjv.3: And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.
kjv.4: And he saith unto them, Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? But they held their peace.
kjv.5: And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
kjv.6: And the Pharisees went forth, and straightway took counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
kjv.7: But Jesus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judaea,
kjv.8: And from Jerusalem, and from Idumaea, and from beyond Jordan; and they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they had heard what great things he did, came unto him.
kjv.9: And he spake to his disciples, that a small ship should wait on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.
kjv.10: For he had healed many; insomuch that they pressed upon him for to touch him, as many as had plagues.
kjv.11: And unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.
kjv.12: And he straitly charged them that they should not make him known.
kjv.13: And he goeth up into a mountain, and calleth unto him whom he would: and they came unto him.
kjv.14: And he ordained twelve, that they should be with him, and that he might send them forth to preach,
kjv.15: And to have power to heal sicknesses, and to cast out devils:
kjv.17: And James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he surnamed them Boanerges, which is, The sons of thunder:
kjv.18: And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Canaanite,
kjv.19: And Judas Iscariot, which also betrayed him: and they went into an house.
kjv.20: And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
kjv.21: And when his friends heard of it, they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is beside himself.
kjv.22: And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils.
kjv.23: And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?
kjv.24: And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
kjv.25: And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
kjv.26: And if Satan rise up against himself, and be divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.
kjv.27: No man can enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he will first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.
kjv.28: Verily I say unto you, All sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:
kjv.29: But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost hath never forgiveness, but is in danger of eternal damnation:
kjv.30: Because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.
kjv.31: There came then his brethren and his mother, and, standing without, sent unto him, calling him.
kjv.32: And the multitude sat about him, and they said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee.
kjv.33: And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?
kjv.34: And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
kjv.35: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.

notes.title: ἐποίησεν δώδεκα — he "made" twelve?
notes.body:
Literally Jesus **"made"** (ἐποίησεν) twelve. English versions say "appointed," "ordained" (KJV), or "chose." ποιέω has a broad range, and "appoint/institute" is well attested (cf. LXX 1 Samuel 12:6, "the LORD who *made* Moses and Aaron" = installed them in office; Hebrews 3:2).

The lesson: a lemma's **gloss is not its meaning** — meaning is selected by context from the word's attested range. Some also hear an echo of creation language (Mark says he made twelve "*that they might be with him*"), but don't over-read: "appointed" is doing normal Greek work here.

Note the textual wrinkle: some early manuscripts add "whom he also named apostles" (probably harmonized from Luke 6:13); compare your English versions.

notes.title: οἱ παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ — "his family" went out to seize him?
notes.body:
Literally "**those from beside him**" — an idiom whose referent must be inferred: his relatives/family (most likely, cf. v. 31 where his mother and brothers arrive), or more loosely his associates/friends.

And what did they say? ἔλεγον ὅτι **ἐξέστη** — "he is out of his mind / beside himself." Two decisions stack up:

1. **Who**: "his family" (most modern versions) vs "his friends" (KJV) — bolder vs safer.
2. **Who said it**: ἔλεγον could be "they [the family] were saying" or impersonal, "people were saying" — some translators soften the scandal of Jesus' family thinking him mad by choosing the impersonal reading.

A verse where lexicon, grammar, and theological discomfort visibly pull on the translation — compare your versions and watch them squirm.
